creator: rehsack@googlemail.com
text:
After a hard disk crash I had to update my FreeBSD 7-STABLE (last updated end of May 2009) to the current STABLE (I always used -STABLE since FreeBSD 4).

As usual, I did csup, rebuild kernel & world, installs and reboot. After I plugged an USB stick, the kernel panics.

I tried at another machine, which has been configured newly from the scratch - there the umass plug in doesn't crash the machine. The machines differ in hardware configuration - the crashing one is a Intel configuration (Dual Xeon), the non-crashing an AMD/VIA chipset based notebook.

If my kernel configuration would help, I'll send it in a separate mail - but I hope the backtrace is more helpful.

Fix: Patch attached with submission follows:
